_meta.note: QC: ~75w, popular-science register, B2-C1 vocab so mechanical blanks (every other word from sentence 2) land at real difficulty (~5.7 chars).

items.passage: Many birds undertake remarkable journeys each year, traveling thousands of kilometers between their breeding and wintering grounds. These migrations are triggered largely by changes in daylight, which signal the approaching season. To navigate, birds rely on a combination of the sun, the stars, and the Earth's magnetic field. Remarkably, the young of some species complete their first journey alone, guided entirely by instinct rather than learned experience.

Volcanic islands form where molten rock rises through the ocean floor and gradually accumulates above the surface of the water. Over thousands of years, repeated eruptions build the island higher and broaden its base considerably. Once the volcano becomes dormant, erosion slowly wears the exposed rock away, while coral may establish itself along the warm surrounding shores. Eventually some islands subside entirely, leaving only a circular reef behind.

The printing press transformed European society after its introduction in the fifteenth century. Before its invention, books were copied laboriously by hand, which made them scarce and extraordinarily expensive. The new technology allowed identical copies to be produced rapidly and distributed widely across the continent. As a result, literacy spread, new ideas circulated more freely, and established authorities gradually lost their tight control over knowledge.
